human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG category-operations specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, customer-impact control, traceability and release boundary handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG category execution, item-master and category-content readiness, inventory and lot-aware activation, blocked-work escalation, and completion evidence.

Authority sources cover FDA preventive controls, FDA and eCFR cGMP expectations, FDA supplier-program, traceability, labeling, and recall boundaries, plus GS1 traceability, GTIN, and data-synchronization standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, strategy and pricing boundary rejection, traceability conflicts, quality holds, unsupported completion, conflicting-policy escalation, supplier substitution pressure, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/category-operations-specialist/ so category operations remain distinct from adjacent quality, planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, logistics, and commercial strategy lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around assortment strategy, pricing approval, supplier approval, regulatory interpretation, final QA release, and logistics release authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG category operations specialist for category readiness, item and content activation control, blocked-work handling, traceability-aware execution, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
